sections.paragraph:
ἀδιήγητος, ον indescribable (so X. et al.; Ael. Aristid. 33, 30 K.=51 p. 581 D.; PGM 1, 164; EpArist 89; 99; Just., D. 32, 2 [cp. Is 53:8=Ac 8:33]) ὁ ἐν ἀγάπῃ ἀ. a man of inexpressible love IEph 1:3.—DELG s.v. ἡγέομαι.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 20
